docs.title: Social LEAP Estimates Animal Poses (SLEAP)
docs.text: SLEAP is an open-source deep-learning based framework for multi-animal pose tracking (Pereira et al., Nature Methods, 2022). It can be used to track any type or number of animals and includes an advanced labeling/training GUI for active learning and proofreading.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Easy, one-line installation with support for all OSes

  • Purpose-built GUI and human-in-the-loop workflow for rapidly labeling large datasets

  • Single- and multi-animal pose estimation with top-down and bottom-up training strategies

  • State-of-the-art pretrained and customizable neural network architectures that deliver accurate predictions with very few labels

  • Fast training: 15 to 60 mins on a single GPU for a typical dataset

  • Fast inference: up to 600+ FPS for batch, <10ms latency for realtime

  • Support for remote training/inference workflow (for using SLEAP without GPUs)

  • Flexible developer API for building integrated apps and customization

docs.title: Get some SLEAP
docs.text:
SLEAP is installed as a Python package. We strongly recommend using Miniconda to install SLEAP in its own environment.

You can find the latest version of SLEAP in the Releases page.

Documentation for New SLEAP Versions

This documentation is for the latest version of SLEAP. If you are using SLEAP version 1.4.1 or earlier, please visit the legacy documentation.

docs.title: Quick install
docs.text:
conda (Windows/Linux/GPU):

conda create -y -n sleap -c conda-forge -c nvidia -c sleap/label/dev -c sleap -c anaconda sleap
pip (any OS except Apple silicon):

 pip install sleap

See the docs for full installation instructions.

docs.title: Learn to SLEAP
docs.text:
• Learn step-by-step: Tutorial
  • Learn more advanced usage: Guides and Notebooks
  • Learn by watching: ABL:AOC 2023 Workshop and MIT CBMM Tutorial
  • Learn by reading: Paper (Pereira et al., Nature Methods, 2022) and Review on behavioral quantification (Pereira et al., Nature Neuroscience, 2020)
  • Learn from others: Discussions on Github

docs.title: References
docs.text:
SLEAP is the successor to the single-animal pose estimation software LEAP (Pereira et al., Nature Methods, 2019).

If you use SLEAP in your research, please cite:

T.D. Pereira, N. Tabris, A. Matsliah, D. M. Turner, J. Li, S. Ravindranath, E. S. Papadoyannis, E. Normand, D. S. Deutsch, Z. Y. Wang, G. C. McKenzie-Smith, C. C. Mitelut, M. D. Castro, J. D’Uva, M. Kislin, D. H. Sanes, S. D. Kocher, S. S-H, A. L. Falkner, J. W. Shaevitz, and M. Murthy. Sleap: A deep learning system for multi-animal pose tracking. Nature Methods, 19(4), 2022

docs.title: Contact
docs.text:
Follow @talmop on X for news and updates!

Technical issue with the software?

  1. Check the Help page.
  2. Ask the community via discussions on Github.
  3. Search the issues on GitHub or open a new one.

General inquiries? Reach out to talmo@salk.edu.

docs.title: Contributors
docs.text:
• Talmo Pereira, Salk Institute for Biological Studies
  • Liezl Maree, Salk Institute for Biological Studies
  • Arlo Sheridan, Salk Institute for Biological Studies
  • Arie Matsliah, Princeton Neuroscience Institute, Princeton University
  • Nat Tabris, Princeton Neuroscience Institute, Princeton University
  • David Turner, Research Computing and Princeton Neuroscience Institute, Princeton University
  • Joshua Shaevitz, Physics and Lewis-Sigler Institute, Princeton University
  • Mala Murthy, Princeton Neuroscience Institute, Princeton University

SLEAP was created in the Murthy and Shaevitz labs at the Princeton Neuroscience Institute at Princeton University.

SLEAP is currently being developed and maintained in the Talmo Lab at the Salk Institute for Biological Studies, in collaboration with the Murthy and Shaevitz labs at Princeton University.

This work was made possible through our funding sources, including:

  • NIH BRAIN Initiative R01 NS104899
  • Princeton Innovation Accelerator Fund

docs.title: License
docs.text: SLEAP is released under a Clear BSD License and is intended for research/academic use only. For commercial use, please contact: Laurie Tzodikov (Assistant Director, Office of Technology Licensing), Princeton University, 609-258-7256.

  • Menu reorganization - Instance selection moved to Go menu - Fit instances to view moved to top of View menu - Render clip moved to View menu - Instance, prediction and track manipulation moved to Labels menu - Added more options for exporting packages - Add links to documentation, code and website to Help menu - Add release checking to Help menu
  • Release checker - Added new version checking through the GitHub REST API
  • Add the ability to export image data for custom frames and suggestions - Labels.save and labels.save (instance method) can now embed the user labeled frames, all labeled frames (including empty ones), and/or suggested frames (which don't have associated LabeledFrames). This makes it easier to export frames for inference together with the ones used for training.
  • Add convenience methods for interacting with exported packages - HDF5Videos now have has_embedded_images, source_video_available, and embedded_frame_inds properties to check for embedded images - HDF5Video now auto-detects the exported package format, indicated by having sub-datasets named "/video" and "/frame_numbers" - Tests
  • Bump alpha version
  • Add tests for fit instance rect detection
  • Training config validation in the GUI - Add validation fraction to config editor - Remove output stride from backbone config UI - Infer output stride from heads - Constrain max stride from heads - Disable tensorboard logging by default - Disable OHKM by default - Correctly detect multiple built-in profiles
  • Update built-in baseline profiles - Remove dataset specific fields (e.g., "anchor_part") - Add medium/large RF variants - Remove unused profiles - Disable tensorboard logging by default - Standardize optimization parameters
  • Add head type to the run name suffix when saving a training pipeline - Prevents multi-model training jobs from being overwritten (#415)
  • Keep previous state when fit to zoom is enabled with no instances - Addresses use case of when user is zoomed to instances but then seeks back/forward to frames with no predictions (#416)
  • Bump alpha version
  • Minor codestyle fixes and update deprecated attrs constructor
  • Set h5py required version to 2.10 to prevent breaking tf.keras in 3.0
  • Add maDLC labels import support

  • Formatting and style

  • Add describe method to Labels for easy inspection of dataset stats

  • Add nn.data.pipelines and nn.inference to toplevel imports
  • Add range specification of example indices to VideoReader
  • Add pipeline creation method to sleap.Video - Creates a VideoReader-based pipeline from the Video instance with optional batching, prefetching and frame index specification - InferenceModel.predict() will automatically call this method if predicting on a sleap.Video
  • Version fence numpy due to TF 2.3 requirements
  • Add base InferenceLayer and InferenceModel classes - These handle all preprocessing and variable size output batching during inference. - Add docstrings and tests for new topdown classes. - Automatically infer model output stride. - Enable local offset refinement for peak finding.
  • Add option to convert unragged examples to numpy arrays - Uses the same utility function as tf.keras.Model.predict
  • Revert colorspace normalization to using static shapes
  • Add bounds for confmaps viz
  • Clean up and unify peak finding methods - find_local_peaks and find_global_peaks now work as single entrypoint and allow specification of the refinement method - Re-enabled quarter pixel local-offset refinement
  • Fix for global peak finding with integral regression - Fixes edge case where the first peak coordinate is NaN results in crash when cropping local patches. - Add test for edge case.
  • Update conda.yml
  • Create conda.yml
  • Change model construction to create flat list of output tensors. - Fixes bottom-up training. - Necessary in order to keep shapes lined up with data generator. - Head tensor specification should just refer to the output tensor names explicitly to avoid ambiguous nesting/ordering.
  • Clean up topdown predictor classmethod creation
  • Fix evaluate_model saving metrics even if save=False is specified
  • Separate topdown model creation from loader classmethod. - Allows for creation of the predictor from memory. - Automatically created when running predict() if not already provided.
  • Fix grouping dtype sometimes being incorrect when using GT instances
  • Remove duplicate PyPI requirements from environment.yml - These get pulled in through setup.py -> requirements.txt.
  • Fix broken normalizer test - Switched to using static shapes, which should be known from the provider anyway. - Unclear what the cause is, but it seems that dynamic shapes can't be evaluated properly for inferring normalization mode, at least in the test example.
  • Fix topdown training visualization
  • Docstrings and tests for FindInstancePeaks - Now supports raw tensors without being in an example. - Added tests with a dummy model.
  • Peak finding fixes for cases with no peaks - Added tests for main peak finding methods
  • Fix topdown model when max_instances IS specified
  • Fix topdown model when max_instances is not specified
  • Set image shape explicitly in providers. - Necessary in some pipeline configurations where the shape may need to be specified for certain ops like resizing.
  • Make sure unrag_tensor uses the right dtype for calculating the shape
  • Fix ground truth instance peaks layer - Now matches sample-wise and matches the correct number of centroids - Add more tests to catch this case
  • Implement new topdown ground truth layers - Refactoring of nn.inference and docstrings - Tests - Edge case still needs to be fixed: matching in ground truth instance peaks layer will duplicate instances to match bounding shape. This results in one broken test for tracking.
  • Unragging functions for converting ragged tensors to concrete - unrag_example: unrags all items in a dict to their bounding shape. - unrag_tensor: unrag a single tensor with possibly multiple ragged dimensions and with the ability to specify the max size to pad to. - Add option to leave batched examples as ragged tensors in Batcher
  • Add general purpose utility for describing tensors - Use in pipeline.describe()
  • Add pipeline peek and describe methods - QOL additions to make it easier to inspect the outputs of pipelines when composing custom ones.
  • Specify tensor shapes for variable length values in LabelsProvider - Needed in TF 2.3 for ragged batching
  • QOL: Better string representations for some of the core data structures - Labels, LabeledFrame and Skeleton now show counts instead of spamming the full default attrs repr and str.
  • Add slice indexing to labels
  • Enable saving labels packages with images from frames without instances - Makes it possible to save extra images, e.g., for inference or for use in semi-supervised learning contexts.
  • Single model topdown implementation. - Centroid cropping and instance prediction all in one tf.keras.Model! - Results in massive speed-up over the tf.data.Dataset-based inference with separate models. - Still missing version with ground truth for mocking sub-components.
  • Color by track when plotting labeled frame via lf.plot()
  • Extend image resizing and padding to work with rank 4 (batched) images.
  • Remove old implementations of peak finding functions for transformers
  • Re-implement general purpose peak finders. - These are a little redundant, but enable the fast implementation of local and global peak finding using integral regression with batches. - This replaces the implementations in nn.data.inference. - Fixes batched global peak finding bug.
  • Parametrize device for KeyMover transformer - Remove debug print statements
  • Remove unused conda-cpu build config
  • Update tests for TF2.3 - Fix layer name test: tf.keras.layers.Add sometimes gets instantiated as a direct op? - Fix Batcher to check for ragged tensor type before conversion. This is because tf.data.experimental.dense_to_ragged_batch will now leave elements as tf.Tensors if shape is fixed and known.
  • Update to TensorFlow 2.3 - Replace tensorflow CUDA packages with direct cudatoolkit/cudnn since we install tensorflow from pypi anyway. - Update AppVeyor build script hack to ignore conda cuda packages for lower build times.

docs.title: v1.0.10a9 (2020-11-16)
docs.text:
• Bump alpha version
  • Update the GUI skeleton after inference if it changed (#414)
  • fix flow tracker failing at flow_shift_instances()
  • Hotfix for backbone output stride detection - This will soon be handled differently/removed from the config entirely

docs.title: v1.0.10a8 (2020-11-06)
docs.text:
• Bump prerelease version
  • Fix new export commands
  • Menu reorganization - Instance selection moved to Go menu - Fit instances to view moved to top of View menu - Render clip moved to View menu - Instance, prediction and track manipulation moved to Labels menu - Added more options for exporting packages - Add links to documentation, code and website to Help menu - Add release checking to Help menu
  • Release checker - Added new version checking through the GitHub REST API
  • Add the ability to export image data for custom frames and suggestions - Labels.save and labels.save (instance method) can now embed the user labeled frames, all labeled frames (including empty ones), and/or suggested frames (which don't have associated LabeledFrames). This makes it easier to export frames for inference together with the ones used for training.
  • Add convenience methods for interacting with exported packages - HDF5Videos now have has_embedded_images, source_video_available, and embedded_frame_inds properties to check for embedded images - HDF5Video now auto-detects the exported package format, indicated by having sub-datasets named "/video" and "/frame_numbers" - Tests

docs.title: v1.0.10a7 (2020-11-05)
docs.text:
• Bump alpha version
  • Add tests for fit instance rect detection
  • Training config validation in the GUI - Add validation fraction to config editor - Remove output stride from backbone config UI - Infer output stride from heads - Constrain max stride from heads - Disable tensorboard logging by default - Disable OHKM by default - Correctly detect multiple built-in profiles
  • Update built-in baseline profiles - Remove dataset specific fields (e.g., "anchor_part") - Add medium/large RF variants - Remove unused profiles - Disable tensorboard logging by default - Standardize optimization parameters
  • Add head type to the run name suffix when saving a training pipeline - Prevents multi-model training jobs from being overwritten (#415)
  • Keep previous state when fit to zoom is enabled with no instances - Addresses use case of when user is zoomed to instances but then seeks back/forward to frames with no predictions (#416)

docs.title: v1.0.4a3 (2020-06-17)
docs.text:
• Bump alpha version
  • Fix confidence map generator tests to account for sigma stride scaling
  • Fix upsampling stack tests to account for input intermediate features
  • Fix logging string formatting in evaluate_model.
  • Add prediction and metric evaluation at the end of training procedure.
  • Fix circular imports in evals
  • Remove warning when video paths cannot be found by default. - This reduces sometimes large amount of spam when loading labels projects interactively that have many video files.
  • Fix logging -> logger scope in training.
  • Set default integral refinement patch size to 5. - Slightly faster and sufficient with sigma = 1 in simulation experiments.
  • Add docstrings to evaluation module and add convenience method. - evaluate_model can be used during training to generate predictions and metrics.
  • Confidence map sigmas are now scaled by their output stride. - This enables a consistent spread in crops around peaks for subpixel refinement and reduces quantization error.
  • Add automatic Provider instantiation for Predictor classes.
  • Add run() method to Pipeline as a convenience consumer function. - Returns all examples from a pipeline in a single call.
  • Add numpy() method to Instances analogous to EagerTensors.
  • Include the output of the encoder in intermediate feature list. - Now models that use UpsamplingStack can output to heads with the same output stride as the encoder's max stride, i.e., the features before any upsampling layers. - Fix error message when missing feature activation for a head.
  • Fix model construction with no intermediate features
  • Start new track after gap in tracking.
  • Modified the matching algorithm.
  • Exclude invalid matches.
  • Improvements to gui inference frame list support

docs.text:

  • Fix logging string formatting in evaluate_model.
  • Add prediction and metric evaluation at the end of training procedure.
  • Fix circular imports in evals
  • Remove warning when video paths cannot be found by default. - This reduces sometimes large amount of spam when loading labels projects interactively that have many video files.
  • Fix logging -> logger scope in training.
  • Set default integral refinement patch size to 5. - Slightly faster and sufficient with sigma = 1 in simulation experiments.
  • Add docstrings to evaluation module and add convenience method. - evaluate_model can be used during training to generate predictions and metrics.
  • Confidence map sigmas are now scaled by their output stride. - This enables a consistent spread in crops around peaks for subpixel refinement and reduces quantization error.
  • Add automatic Provider instantiation for Predictor classes.
  • Add run() method to Pipeline as a convenience consumer function. - Returns all examples from a pipeline in a single call.
  • Add numpy() method to Instances analogous to EagerTensors.
  • Include the output of the encoder in intermediate feature list. - Now models that use UpsamplingStack can output to heads with the same output stride as the encoder's max stride, i.e., the features before any upsampling layers. - Fix error message when missing feature activation for a head.

  • UNet architecture fixes - Add pooling block at the end of the encoder stack. This is necessary since UNet uses the last conv in each block as the skip source, so the encoder blocks cannot end with a max pooling. Additionally, the first block cannot start with a max pooling on the input, so we add an extra pooling-only block at the end of the encoder stack to compensate. - Middle block implemented. This block adds convolutions without pooling at the end of the encoder stack. It increases the number of filters, and then decreases back to the input filter count. When set to True, the model construction is exactly identical to the legacy models. - Added comment about middle block to config. - Fixed incorrect upsampling blocks: should not have batch norm and the filters after the first refinement conv should decrease (within the block). - Implemented several of the functionalities required above in the simple encoder/decoder blocks.

  • More logging in Trainer.
  • Major clean up of sleap.nn. - Remove all unused or orphaned modules or functions. - Add basic module-level docstrings. - Minor formatting - Import most submodules from top level sleap init (adds only ~400ms startup time to "import sleap")
  • Full inference pipelines implemented for topdown and bottomup. - Both can be initialized from just run paths. - Both have a .predict() method that creates LabeledFrames on demand. - Both have a .predict_generator() method that generates raw examples for flexibility in postprocessing.
  • Expand transformers for topdown postprocessing - PredictedInstanceCropper and PredictedCenterInstanceNormalizer now pass forward centroid and peak confidences.
  • Additional plotting/visualization utilities
  • Add from_arrays convenience classmethod for PredictedInstances
  • Add videos attribute to providers for consistent video lookup
  • Add PointsResizer transformer to account for scale during inference
  • Rewrote PAF grouping module to for data pipeline inference - PartAffinityFieldInstanceGrouper is the transformer - Pretty under-commented and incomplete typing - PAFScorer should definitely be broken up somehow
  • Run data pipeline inference ops on the GPU if possible
  • Add best_logical_device_name utility - Returns the best initialized (logical) device name for use with tf.device for explicit tensor device placement.
  • Adjust batch/repeat ordering based on number of samples in the data
  • Fix part names not being stored in the config for MultiInstance models
  • Training/validation split from val fraction config in training
  • sleap.Labels splitting utility
  • Convenience attribute for building the run path in config
  • Add more logging to Trainer and disable PAF quiver plot - Quiver plot has a huge memory leak on the matplotlib side right now
  • Initial inference pipeline implementation - Topdown with centroid + confmap models - Moved old inference.py until deprecated
  • Add single peak-set plotting with colormap cycling
  • Resizer with optional point scaling since GT is not always available
  • VideoReader provider returns video_ind
  • Clean up inference and add topdown-related inference transformers
  • Unbatcher dataset transformer
  • Remove remaining Union-style configs to enable structuring from file - Also fix the global structuring hook for Skeleton
  • Import sleap.nn.config and .system at top level
  • Added summary method to nn.system module
  • Use sleap.nn.system.use_cpu_only in nn tests
  • Added utility functions for querying and setting GPU availability - Check for GPUs available - Set which GPUs to use (or CPU only) - Set memory preallocation policy - Check for initialization - Explicit device initialization
  • Minor formatting
  • Delete unused or replaced modules/tests
  • Timing in setup logging, typing, docstrings and auto-scale visualization
  • Remove unnecessary matplotlib backend specification. - Will probably need to check that this still works across platforms, or provide a parameter to set this explicitly.
  • Add explicit key arg for global peak finder in topdown pipeline builder.
  • nn.training.Trainer implementation. - Handles centroids, topdown and multi-instance model types. - Output type specific trainers: CentroidConfmapsModelTrainer, TopdownConfmapsModelTrainer, BottomUpModelTrainer - CLI with behavior mostly equivalent to old format. Run path should be fully specified since it'll be harder to parse from stdout. - Basic docstrings - Several small functions for setting up the training still need to be rolled into Trainer, or maybe a TrainingJob that sits between the config and the actual trainer.
  • Add serialization/deserialization helper methods to TrainingJobConfig. - Need to re-implement the legacy format parser when unstructuring.
  • Add docstrings to nn.model and expose keras model as attribute. - Now Model.make_model(...) also sets that instance's keras_model attribute for convenience. - Class method constructor from config can optionally update the input config object with argument or inferred data.
  • Visualization fixes - Squeeze singleton image channel (grayscale) - Explicit control of paired peak line plotting - Formatting
  • Add visualization utilities
  • Replace configs that could be set to "auto" with None (#307)
  • Add range for 'double' form fields.
  • Add methods to set form data for stack widget.
  • Fix reduction in multi-PAF generation
  • Misc changes to FormBuilder
  • Add TextOrListWidget for text/list widget.
  • Add OptionalSpinWidget for optional int/doubles.
  • Add loss_weight to bottom up configuration and expand nn.head docstrings
  • Completed docstrings in nn.config.model for the output head types.
  • Implement data pipelines for the 3 major model types - Implements several versions of each pipeline: - base_pipeline: Fetches input data with no optimization. Useful for composing with other pipelines and for quick inspection. - training_pipeline: Generates examples with all data structures needed for training. Uses optimizations to minimize I/O latency across iterations like preloading/prefetching. Applies data augmentation and shuffling. - viz_pipeline: Produces minimal examples necessary for generating visualizations of predictions during training. Necessarily, this implements basic inference, but does not have some optimizations that a full inference pipeline might benefit from. - Lots of repetition but keeping it as-is for now for flexibility
  • Bug fixes in KeyMapper and enforcement of static shapes - Should probably look into this issue more closely since it breaks dynamic shaped inputs (could be an issue for multi-instance inference potentially?)
  • Add flatten_channels option to PAF generator for convenience
  • Rename MultiInstanceConfig.multi_instance -> .confmaps
  • Fix UNet stem construction - Previously the first stem block started with a max pooling directly on the input layer
  • Implement transformer for local peak finding
  • Flip key names for confidence maps vs centroids in generator
  • Typo in model creation from intermediate tensor
  • Preprocessing config docstring typo
  • Initial nn.training module refactoring - Moved old training module to *_legacy.py for now - Added several generic builder functions. These will likely be replaced by the TrainingJob/Trainer classes, but writing them out now to get a sense of where we need coupling.
  • Add missing import for instance cropping utility
  • Clean up callbacks imports and add typing for plotting callbacks
  • Instance crop size estimation utility function - Should probably get moved somewhere else when nn is cleaned up
  • Created initial transformer classes for inference - KerasModelPredictor supports multi-input/output and runs extremely fast on GPU (but the device can be set at the class-level). - Refactored global peak finding to return coordinates in points format and apply thresholding before returning. - GlobalPeakFinder transformer for generic peak finding on any confmaps key with stride adjustment.
  • Create nn.losses module for custom losses/metrics - Created standalone OHKM loss computation function for easier testing. - Added heuristics for ratio-based dynamic hard keypoint detection. - Fix default OHKM ratio config (should be >1)
  • Add profile_graph to TensorBoard configuration. - Removed part_metrics since this will always be enabled.
  • Update ZMQ callback class syntaxes and add ModelCheckpoint event
  • Add global structuring hooks for sleap.Skeleton - This allows for (un)structuring instances without needing to write any extra code for instances nested in larger attrs classes.
  • Import architecture classes in nn.architectures
  • Refactor nn.heads and nn.model - classmethods for instantiation from config classes - Moved old modules to *_legacy.py until TrainingJob conversion is done - Several tweaks to configs
  • Add KeyMapper to nn.data.pipelines
  • oneof decorator injects a which_oneof method to retrieve the set attrib
  • ResNet from config
  • Upsampling stack from config and explicit control of skips
  • Hourglass from config
  • LEAP from config
  • Refactor Unet -> UNet and implement construction from config.
  • Implement oneof decorator to enforce mutual exclusivity - This achieves validation to enforce the behavior desired for the mutually-exclusive attribute classes. - Classes decorated with attr.s and oneof will raise an error if more than one attribute is set to not None when instantiated.
  • Add old config module docstring back in.
  • Split nn.config.py into submodules for legibility + initial ModelConfig - Config sections now sit in their own submodules, but the syntax is the same since all config classes are imported in nn/config/init.py. - Initial implementation of ModelConfig. Totally decoupled from previous Model/ModelConfig implementations. - High level architecture parameters are now explicitly specified for each architecture and "oneof"-style options will just be specified by having different attributes which validators can check for mutual exclusivity. This should make it easier to serialize into more readable json as opposed to the old system of specifying the class name and doing some voodoo to construct the correct one. - This decoupling comes at the cost of reduced flexibility in model customization (though this can overcome with "generic" model configs like EncoderDecoder), as well as requiring manual specification of parameters for each architecture.
  • Add attribute for specifying UNet stem kernel size. - Useful for increasing receptive field without adding more blocks. - Defaults still match the canonical architecture and tests.
  • Major nn refactor in preparation for new Model/ModelConfig classes - Cleaned up nn.architectures to remove architectures that need major updating before they can be ported, removed unused utility functions. - We'll port in the deleted architectures from other branches when we're ready, but in the meanwhile they can't be used without major rewrites. - Exception: hrnet module which is not pretrained and will require minor adjustments to fit new format. - Replace old nn.model module with nn.model_config - Removed PreprocessingConfig from nn.model since that's covered elsewhere in nn.config.DataConfig. - Removed preprocessing-related args from legacy classmethod builders in ModelConfig.
  • Move AugmentationConfig to nn.config module
  • Resizing from config
  • Normalizer from config
  • More flexible concatenation operators for Pipeline
  • Basic pipeline concatenation
  • InstanceCentroidFinder from config
  • Instance cropping formatting.
  • Instance cropping from config.
  • Pipeline attributes default to empty lists. - This is necessary to build partial pipelines without providers.
  • Initial nn.config implementation. - This contains simple attr attribute classes with all user-facing configuration options related to training. - This supersedes the previous TrainingJob class and should contain all serializable job-related parameters. - Functional modules should be able to be instantiated from these, but can be decoupled in the actual implementation.
  • Add LabelsReader splitting utility function
  • Added example_indices attribute to providers. - Replaces the ds_index argument to the make_dataset method - Allows for flexible specification of subsets of the data to be used without having to create new source data objects (e.g., sleap.Labels). This makes it easier to read subsets of videos or to split the labels for train/val/test sets. - Examples generated by LabelsReader now include an "example_ind" key to indicate the index of the example within the original data.
  • Initial data.training module for training-related data ops
  • Tests for dataset_ops
  • Preloader now stores elements in an attribute.
  • Add comment about preloading cost and add as option to pipelines.
  • Fix stochastic deadlocks in tf.py_functions. - These resulted random hangs during training that could not be interrupted via SIGINT or otherwise. - Solution was to remove TF ops from inside py_functions in the data pipeline. - Interesting side effect is a big performance speedup. - See: https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorflow/issues/32454
  • Create Preloader transformer. - Loads elements of the pipeline at creation time rather than during execution. This is a workaround to weird thread safety issues with using Labels as a provider during training.
  • Filter irrelevant kwargs from model backbone.
  • Add pipelines submodule to nn.data.
  • Use new pipeline in datagen test.
  • Formatting and commenting out unused attributes for now
  • Fix truncation rounding error during instance cropping. - float32 precision sometimes results in bounding box sizes that are #.9997, which can result in an incorrect crop, even though the centered bounding boxes are created with exact dimensions.
  • Add initial generic pipelines for generating training data. - BottomUpPipeline: for building standard multi-confmap + PAF pipelines - TopDownPipeline: for building standard instance cropped pipelines
  • Dataset operations transformers module - Transformers that operate over multiple elements of the data: - Shuffler - Batcher (this one is tricky with variable length elements) - Prefetcher
  • Initial pipeline builders implementation
  • Fix Resizer not applying scaling to points.
  • Add Resizer transformer
  • Add missing documentation to Normalizer
  • Add Normalizer transformer
  • Remove debugging comments
  • Add dev_requirements.txt to environment.yml
  • Add editable package install to conda environment.yml
  • Add PAF generator class and tests
  • Edge maps transformers module. - Implements edge confidence maps; equivalent of part confidence maps where scalar values are scaled by the distance of the sampling grid points to the line segment formed by edges. - Implement part affinity fields as simple unit vectors weighted by the edge confidence maps for masking.
  • Fix expand_to_rank when rank is higher than the target. - Add test.
  • Add data module level init.py
  • Remove indentation on blank lines
  • Fixes and tests for normalization
  • Remove indentation on blank lines
  • Move make_grid_vectors to nn.data.utils as it's reused across modules.
  • Resizing transformers module
  • Missing import in utils and added tests
  • imgaug/numpy monkey patch for some version combinations. - Track issue: https://github.com/aleju/imgaug/issues/537
  • Add rank expansion data utility.
  • Initial data normalization method implementations. - Documented the normalization modes expected when using different pretrained models from tf.keras.applications. - Still needs tests and a transformer block class.
  • Modified the "scale" key to a 2-tuple indicating the x- and y-scaling. - The general expectation is that points in individual examples are already on the image grid scale. - The "scale" key can be used to track scaling operations, including aspect ratio changes. This information is sufficient to map points on example images/confidence maps to the raw images. - Commented scaling behavior across pipeline modules. - Changed behavior of MultiConfidenceMaps to compute confidence maps on the current image grid under the assumption that the "instances" key is at the same scale as the "image".
  • Rename instance cropping image size to "full_image_*" - "Full" to indicate it refers to the input to the instance cropper, which may have been modified relative to the "raw" image, e.g., due to padding/scaling. - Rename attribute drop_full_image to keep_full_image
  • Store raw image size in examples generated by data providers. - Useful for downstream processing that affects shape.
  • Add confidence map generation transformers. - Low level confidence map generation functions - Multi-instance confidence map generator - Multi-instance centroid confidence map generator - Instance-cropped center instance confidence map generator - Instance-cropped all instance confidence map generator - Tests
  • Add instance cropping transformer. - Agnostic to centroid calculation method. - Propagates all frame example metadata to individual instance examples. - Tests for coordinate transformations correctness, especially for tricky bounding box and cropping calculations.
  • Remove unnecessary import from instance centroid tests.
  • Add instance centroid finder transformer. - Implements bbox midpoint and anchor part-based centroid finding. This can later be extended to include other centroid computation methods like backup anchors, median, mean, etc. - Add base data pipeline utils module.
  • Remove unnecessary import from augmentation test
  • Transformer for data augmentation via imgaug - Separate AugmentationConfig class, possibly to be exported for serialization of the configuration.
  • Remove old nn.data module and start new data pipeline implementation. - The new modules will generalize the data processing steps to make it easier to test, re-use code and optimally compose tf.data-based pipelines. - Individual blocks will specify their input/output requirements such that dataset examples will know what to expect and can be validated at a higher level. - Enables attrs-based classes for configuring individual blocks. - Added initial data providers from Labels and raw videos, unifying both training and inference data loading.
  • Incomplete condition for preprocessing config
  • ModelConfig completeness properties
  • Add missing imports for json loading
  • Update ModelConfig for new OutputHead format - Expanded PreprocessingConfig to account for all current preprocessing steps, with checks for required parameters for training vs inference - Additional methods for initialization of ModelConfig from legacy formats. Almost all parameters can now be retrieved from old training job files.
  • Type annotation for is_complete outputs
  • Make run/dataset-specific head attributes optional - OutputTypes now have an is_complete attribute to indicate whether sufficient information has been provided to be able to instantiate the layers. - Can now structure generic output heads without dataset specific information (e.g., skeletons) to allow for specification of "template" configurations
  • Initial implementation of new model configuration module. - Support for structuring from legacy file format. - Initial tests.
  • Add validator for OutputHead type vs config.
  • Add necessary properties for valid backbone architectures. - maximum_stride, output_stride and make_backbone are sufficient to specify the necessary information for preprocessing data before the backbone and to generate the tensors using a common interface.
  • Add centered attribute to SinglePartConfmaps for topdown mode.
  • Remove part_names requirement for PartAffinityFields head.
  • Additional test for centroid confmap configuration without args
  • Expanded validation of output head configuration.
  • Broke up architecture tests into subtests for cleaner debugging.
  • Initial implementation of generic model head types.
  • Fix point-only dataset generator from concrete arrays.
  • Fix available architectures.
  • Implement hourglass backbone. - Add skip connection from stem output to the input of each stack's decoder path. This enables recovery of features from the input. - Fix Unet tests with new skip. - Fix available architectures.
  • Bump requirements to TensorFlow 2.1 - Removed conda package in favor of pip which is better maintained - Switch to tensorflow instead of tensorflow-gpu as CUDA deps are now included in the main package, but works for non-GPU systems as well - Windows: Requires MSVC 2019: https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/help/2977003/the-latest-supported-visual-c-downloads
  • Generalize to stacked encoder-decoder architectures. - Added stem to Unet for initial downsampling.
  • Fix Unet symmetry without middle block
  • Re-implement Unet using EncoderDecoder class
  • Add more options to encoder blocks to control skip connection sources - Enable conv block without pooling - Enable pooling before convs within a block - Account for pooling flag when calculating encoder stride - Keep only the first intermediate features at each encoder stride for forming skip connections - Corrected decoder stride calculation when searching for source features
  • Implement encoder-decoder meta-architecture - Created base classes for generalized encoder-decoder architecture. These provide a common API and building blocks for architectures like LEAP CNN, UNet, stacked hourglass, FC-DenseNet and more. - Re-implemented LEAP CNN using EncoderDecoder class. - Test coverage
  • Move IntermediateFeature to sleap.nn.architectures.common
  • More ResNet refactoring - Fix some typing issues - Allow for backbone creation without upsampling stack - Remove default upsampling stack configuration - Fix stem dilation to use correct output stride attribute - Fix output stride calculation - Fix preset variant classes - Add tests
  • Switch to rate-based filter sizes in upsampling stack - Add upsampling stack filter rate tests - Hide visible GPUs for tests
  • Upsampling stack fixes - Fix stride calculation - Fix computed block count dtype - More sensible default for transposed conv filters - Add tests
  • Refactor ResNet implementation - Enable variable output stride for the features backbone with support for pretrained weights via dilated convolution compensation. - Added generic upsampling module to provide common patterns for the decoder portion of architectures. This will be used to refactor and unify the implementation of a variety of architectures. - Changed interface of the backbone to: make_backbone(x_in) -> (x_out, intermediate_outputs) for more flexibility in multi-head configurations. Head instantiation will be done separately upstream in sleap.nn.model.Model creation.
  • Add option to return points with instance confmaps dataset
  • Fix rotation augmentation sign flip
  • Add Keras callback to log matplotlib figures to TensorBoard

docs.title: v0.1.13 (2020-01-29)
docs.text:
• Delay updating caches until all deletes.
  • Bug fix, gen_cm_fn threw if given 3rd arg.
  • Implement visualization saver callback. - Moved visualization plotting from local to Trainer class method. - Added tf.keras callback for saving plots to disk. - Added CLI option to enable visualization saving (similar to --tensorboard).
  • Change default OKS sigma for evaluation function to human data minimum.
  • Add longer trail len options (100, 200, 500)
  • Improved trail code, set to use single node.
  • Pass skeleton for instance_crop_ctr_node_ind.
  • Add instance_crop_ctr_node_ind field.
  • Support for optional_index list.
  • Remove model-specific training params from form.
  • Comment helper function for TensorBoard plotting.
  • Typo in node loss output type check
  • Implement OHKM and node-wise losses - Allows for dynamic tuning of error weight by channel. - Node-wise loss can be computed as a metric for monitoring training progress by body part. - Add training job options to enable new features. - Loaded models now are not compiled. This enables custom losses to be saved without requiring to be available in the loaded context.
  • Select spawned track instance on nav to next spawn
  • Better track trails in gui.
  • Bug fix, don't clear cache if doesn't exist.
  • Use nodes instead of edges for gui inst bbox.
  • Cache counts of user/predicted frames.
  • Scope logging by data split
  • Quick and dirty visualization in TensorBoard

docs.title: v0.1.6 (2019-11-27)
docs.text:
• Remove -gpu from tensorflow requirement in setup.

  • Exclude tensorflow-gpu from setup requirements.
  • Add jsmin to pip packages inside conda sleap pkg.
  • Bug fix when making frames from predictions.
  • Remove keras from conda build env.
  • Use tensorflow 2.0 from conda.
  • Move minimal training profiles into examples dir.
  • Send model type via zmq as string.
  • Sort dict with instances for frames in chunk.
  • Reset run_name before training (not always).
  • More robust message receiving.
  • Add (back) training zmq, run_name in cli.
  • Integrate tf2 nn code with gui.
  • Use new inference modules for overlays.
  • Get hdf5 dataset dtype from test frame.
  • Remove support for hdf5 confmap/paf overlays.
  • Bug fix to eb4ee45cd, pass model paths.
  • Add find_files_by_suffix function.
  • Bug fix, don't overwrite instance_box_length arg.
  • Bug fix to is_trained property.
  • Add node_names to tracking h5.
  • Bug fix, re-enable contextual menu on mouse move.
  • Validate RegionProposalExtractor.merged_box_length
  • Bug fixes for when 0 or 1 peak connections found.
  • Ensure region.merged_box_length > 0.
  • Add cli for running tracking by itself.
  • Hacky bug fix, MediaVideo accepts HDF5 attributes.
  • New Predictor class with policy-based pipeline.
  • Fix pretrained NASNetLarge
  • Expose TensorBoard profiling parameter
  • Multi-head averaging for multi-scale output backbones
  • Expand DenseNet multi-head capability - Fix feature sharing across scales and heads
  • HRNet fixes
  • Add ability to pass in additional training callbacks - Added HDF5Video parameters to utils.VideoLoader
  • Add Tensorboard callback to Trainer
  • Fix cli help, use get_package_file.
  • Remove debug printing.
  • Show minimal message during suggestion generation.
  • Bug fix, shift click to complete points.
  • Update promo movie in the readme (finally)
  • Fix instance cropping using reference center node
  • Quick test fix
  • Add minimal training jobs for other model output types
  • Evaluation submodule with OKS and generalized VOC metrics
  • Mock inference classes that pass GT via the same interface
  • Add centroid point dataset for datagen (without confmaps)
  • Fix rounding in region proposal box grouping
  • Region proposal extraction without merging
  • Store skeleton in training job if not specified in file. - Add Skeleton constructor from simple skeleton data - SimpleSkeleton is now JSON serializable - Allow for providing external skeleton when creating InferenceModel
  • Add more printing during training setup. - TODO: switch to logging
  • More casting at the data generators.
  • Remove deprecated submodules. Breaking commit.
  • More aggressive casting during data generation
  • Add minimal centroid training file.
  • Training pipeline configuration - Support for comments in training job json files via jsmin - CSV logging of training history - Callback-based final model saving - Flexible run name generation - Model path checking using new and old pathing scheme - Instance method for TrainingJob saving - Saving jobs with 4 spaces indentation by default - Handle model specification without skeleton: null - Expose most of the useful objects in Trainer as private attributes, allowing for access to model and data even if Trainer.train() is interrupted. - More outputs with trainer verbosity - Allow specifying val/test data in CLI
  • Auto calculate steps per epoch
  • Fix PAF sampling grid yx->xy orientation
  • Static shape fixes to instance cropping
  • Basic training CLI clean up.
  • Add basic training CLI matching old format. - Bonus: sleap-train works now!
  • Expose more UDenseNet parameters and correct docstring.
  • Handle model and list outputting backbones more gracefully in Model. - Use Trainer.verbosity attribute for callbacks as well.
  • Add support for creating TrainingJobs from JSON strings.
  • Centroid dataset generation and working trainer - Add hints and explicitly enforce static shapes in dataset generation - Fix typos in Trainer methods
  • Add image attribute to LabeledFrame for convenient frame fetching - Bug fix to training data generation in Labels
  • Training pipeline implemented for everything but centroid confmaps.
  • Initial datagen and training re-implementation.
  • Expose attribute for number of filters in transposed conv heads.
  • Fix usage of HigherHRNet attributes in backbone construction.
  • Add HigherHRNet and UDenseNet backbones.
  • Create nn.data submodule for tf.data.Dataset-based data generation. - imgaug-based augmentation - Instance cropping from centroid or reference node - Confidence map generated for basic and topdown-style models - PAF generation
  • Small bugfix in pretrained NASNet initialization and MobileNet typo
  • Bug fix, include all frames for deepposekit
  • Bug fix to demo confmap/paf overlay windows.
  • Make points visible for PoseTrack dataset.
  • Add support for PoseTrack varient of COCO dataset.
  • Add support for importing DeepPoseKit and COCO.
  • Add support for importing DeepPoseKit and COCO.
  • Add scale to training dialog.
  • More typing, cache for iou boxes.
  • Refactor duplicate code out of Tracker classes.
  • Bug fix, get number of frames from video.
  • Remove FIXME's (test was right).
  • Make the local/refinement tests pass.
  • Add tests for nn.region_proposal.
  • Note peak-finding test which now fail.
  • Add tests for nn.paf_grouping.
  • Added threshold for minimum required points for spawning new tracks
  • Added generic tracker that operates on points only - Takes any similarity function and matching function - Added similarity functions: IOU and centroid distance
  • Initial implementation of topdown peak finder
  • Add tests for nn.paf_grouping.
  • Hotfix for TrainingJob/Model imports mess
  • Refactor InferenceModel to be simpler layer on top of a tf.keras.Model. - Can still be created from a TrainingJob using a factory class method. - Handles multi-inputs/outputs more generically - Less tied to TrainingJob/backbone construction, instead computes most properties implicitly. - Added a new utility method to replace .predict() without changing the input tensor to allow for arbitrary input sizes. This is handled implicitly now through the model's call() method.
  • Add peak-finding test for all zeros.
  • Revert local peak finding algorithm - New version didn't have a good way to deal with adjacent local peaks - Added iteration based method to deal with per-channel max filtering. This avoids moving a lot of memory on the GPU as with the old "channel packing" method, at the cost of more constraints on autograph since the channel dimension must be statically inferable. - Refactor ConfmapPeakFinder to batch above autographed functions calls.
  • Refactor which calls get autographed in centroid predictor - Now batching occurs above autographed functions to minimize retracing - Exposed more peak finding parameters at base class level
  • Check for training_job.json within model folder when deserializing
  • Typo in utils.VideoLoader.n_chunks property
  • Add integration test for nn.peak_finding.
  • Add tests for nn.peak_finding.
  • Bug fix for getting random prediction frames.
  • Command line utility for merging tracks.
  • Add test for velocity suggestions.
  • Add suggestioned frames based on high pt velocity.
  • Don't break when there are no tracks.
  • Add support for header series as numpy array.
  • Add hog feature suggestion, better bag of features
  • Disable contextual menu when clicking node.
  • Connect app state to instance overlay.
  • Add menu options for new displacement graph.
  • Fix input shape specification when building new backbones
  • Flow shift tracker refactoring - More faithful to original algorithm (no propagating shifted instances) - Matching options: Greedy or Hungarian - Generic instance similarity function - Use best shifted instance in the window by track ID - Filter shifted instances by optical flow scores and ignore instances with few points - Added tracking score field to PredictedInstances (good signal for proofreading) - Added image scaling as preprocessing for optical flow calculation
  • Add keras_applications-based network backbones. - Fix DenseNet implementation and add variants as well as generalized - Add MobileNetV1 and MobileNetV2 - Add NASNet Mobile, Large and generalized
  • PAF grouper refactoring
  • Add property to Skeleton to return edges as pairs of node indices
  • Add method for calc'ing single node displacement.
  • Fix merge conflicts
  • Divisibility check in resizing and extended batched call
  • Intermediate changes in inference modules overhaul - Peak finding redone - Centroid-based region proposals - Video loading via tf.data.Dataset - Initial tests stub via tf.test.TestCase unittests
  • Use bag of words for brisk, parallel per video.
  • Write tracks as tuples to save space.
  • Bug fix, connect state vars to color manager.
  • Sort groups by numeric rather than alpha order.
  • Moved suggestion pipeline into object.
  • Add test for brisk and pipeline.
  • Add default value for brisk thresh.
  • Add test to ensure hdf5 video import works.
  • Add test, remove debug print.
  • Refactor feature-based frame suggestions.
  • Suggestions grouped by video, interleave sortable.
  • Add group interleave sort option for suggestions.
  • Apply node/edge color to skeleton tables.
  • Initialize dicts in init so not shared.
  • Refactor gui coloring code.
  • conda install Pillow, block installing pyqt
  • Rename active learning gui test file.
  • Rename gui/active.py to gui/inference.py
  • Add support for instance placement methods.
  • Bug fixes for new suggestion format.
  • Remove code that was commented out.
  • Rename "active learning" to "training".
  • Re-enable prediction on suggested/random frames.
  • Mode drop-down menus in learning gui dialog.
  • Add black and pre-commit to dev reqs.
  • Store cluster/group id for suggestions.
  • Complete rewrite of core inference modules. - nn.peak_finding: - Global peak finding - Local peak finding via max filtering with threshold - Gaussian smoothing for suppressing spurious local peaks - Centered cropping - Local direction offset-based subpixel peak refinement - Autographed functions for fast graph mode execution of core ops - Generalized high level wrappers with lightweight eager mode control flow (e.g., different flags, defaults, variable outputs, etc.)
  • Enable Tensorboard through CLI and change default profile save path - Training job JSON now defaults to "{run_path}/training_job.json"
  • Use force-directed graph for node placement.
  • Don't die if ~/.sleap/ already exists.
  • Use config files stored in ~/.sleap/.
  • Get frame_range with default empty range.
  • Add test coverage for track-related commands.
  • Add commands for change point location/visibility.
  • Bug fix when setting track from other video.
  • Major refactoring to app gui code.
  • gui refactoring
  • gui refactoring
  • gui refactoring
  • Added a couple of common properties to flow shifted instances
  • Fix to checkpoint every epoch, not batch
  • Fix dynamic preprocessing for ResNet backbone for TF2/graph mode
  • Fix deprecated kwargs for checkpointing through Keras callback
  • gui refactoring
  • gui refactoring
  • gui refactoring (in progress)
  • Bug fix: out of range peaks crashed PAF line integral lookups
  • Temporarily disabled autograph on functions getting retraced often.
  • Refactored peak finding. - Rewrote all processing code in pure TF. - Enabled some autographing, but still need to rework some of the sub-functions to move more processing into graph mode. - Updated predictor to use new peak finding functions.
  • gui refactoring
  • Update to TF 2.0 - Removed standalone Keras dependency - Added conda package dependencies for CUDA Toolkit and cuDNN - Added TF 2.0 as pip dependency
  • gui refactoring
  • Update inference overlay to use InferenceModel

  • Active inference changes - Removed disk serialization of predictions (it was being overwritten after each video anyway) - Progress dialog works - Canceling works gracefully - Broken: asynchronous call so that the GUI remains responsive
  • Major refactoring of inference module - Created a InferenceModel class that holds all of the metadata required to run models from a TrainingJob. This includes input/output scale, skeletons, Keras model loading and caching, variable input shape, normalization and other convenience properties. - Added some generic attributes to the sleap.nn.Model class to allow for storing or inferring output scaling (depends on architecture details, so it can be overloaded in backbones). - sleap.nn.loadmodel module removed. - Predictor refactored to take advantage of InferenceModel caching and reuse multiprocessing pool (fixes: #155). - Previously, models were being reloaded from disk and copies made every time a new crop size was encountered. - Previously, a new pool was being opened on every predict() call. - Refactored DataTransform usage to account for discrepancies between input scale vs output scale vs relative output scale vs scale across models. - Fix: DataTransform-based scaling would always convert images to float64 dtype. - Refactored TensorFlow-based peak finding for GPU execution. - Account for unspecified confmaps tensor at graph creation time. - Fix subpixel refinement algorithm to enable greater resolution than original data scale. - General rework of offset adjustment. - Exposed supersampling parameters (window size, window batching) - Fix tensors being overwritten or dropping out of context - Fixes: #149 - Exposed peak finding options and other inference functionality as Predictor attributes. - Renamed: Instance.visible_points_array -> Instance.points_array - Sensible cropping defaults in profiles and function kwargs. - Indented default profiles for readability. - General unfucking of syntax errors, anti-patterns, typos, etc.

docs.title: v0.1.1 (2019-07-17)

This enables custom losses to be saved without requiring to be available in the loaded context.

  • TrainingJob.is_trained returns False if model is not found - Previously it would throw an exception from TrainingJob.model_path, which would break the inference GUI if there were empty folders from failed runs with existing training json files - Change TrainingJob.model_path final exception type to more appropriate FileNotFoundError

  • Add weight downloading to FAQ

  • Expose TensorBoard profiling parameter
  • Multi-head averaging for multi-scale output backbones

  • HRNet fixes

  • Add Tensorboard callback to Trainer

  • Evaluation submodule with OKS and generalized VOC metrics
  • Mock inference classes that pass GT via the same interface
  • Add centroid point dataset for datagen (without confmaps)
  • Fix rounding in region proposal box grouping
  • Region proposal extraction without merging
  • Store skeleton in training job if not specified in file. - Add Skeleton constructor from simple skeleton data - SimpleSkeleton is now JSON serializable - Allow for providing external skeleton when creating InferenceModel
  • Add more printing during training setup. - TODO: switch to logging
  • More casting at the data generators.
  • Remove deprecated submodules. Breaking commit.
  • More aggressive casting during data generation
  • Add minimal centroid training file.
  • Training pipeline configuration - Support for comments in training job json files via jsmin - CSV logging of training history - Callback-based final model saving - Flexible run name generation - Model path checking using new and old pathing scheme - Instance method for TrainingJob saving - Saving jobs with 4 spaces indentation by default - Handle model specification without skeleton: null - Expose most of the useful objects in Trainer as private attributes, allowing for access to model and data even if Trainer.train() is interrupted. - More outputs with trainer verbosity - Allow specifying val/test data in CLI
  • Auto calculate steps per epoch
  • Fix PAF sampling grid yx->xy orientation
  • Static shape fixes to instance cropping
  • Basic training CLI clean up.
  • Add basic training CLI matching old format. - Bonus: sleap-train works now!
  • Expose more UDenseNet parameters and correct docstring.
  • Handle model and list outputting backbones more gracefully in Model. - Use Trainer.verbosity attribute for callbacks as well.
  • Add support for creating TrainingJobs from JSON strings.
  • Centroid dataset generation and working trainer - Add hints and explicitly enforce static shapes in dataset generation - Fix typos in Trainer methods
  • Add image attribute to LabeledFrame for convenient frame fetching - Bug fix to training data generation in Labels
  • Training pipeline implemented for everything but centroid confmaps.
  • Initial datagen and training re-implementation.
  • Expose attribute for number of filters in transposed conv heads.
  • Fix usage of HigherHRNet attributes in backbone construction.
  • Add HigherHRNet and UDenseNet backbones.
  • Create nn.data submodule for tf.data.Dataset-based data generation. - imgaug-based augmentation - Instance cropping from centroid or reference node - Confidence map generated for basic and topdown-style models - PAF generation
  • Small bugfix in pretrained NASNet initialization and MobileNet typo
  • Bug fix, include all frames for deepposekit
  • Bug fix to demo confmap/paf overlay windows.
  • Make points visible for PoseTrack dataset.
  • Add support for PoseTrack varient of COCO dataset.
  • Add support for importing DeepPoseKit and COCO.
  • Add support for importing DeepPoseKit and COCO.
  • Add scale to training dialog.
  • More typing, cache for iou boxes.
  • Refactor duplicate code out of Tracker classes.
  • Bug fix, get number of frames from video.
  • Remove FIXME's (test was right).
  • Make the local/refinement tests pass.
  • Add tests for nn.region_proposal.
  • Note peak-finding test which now fail.
  • Add tests for nn.paf_grouping.
  • Added threshold for minimum required points for spawning new tracks
  • Added generic tracker that operates on points only - Takes any similarity function and matching function - Added similarity functions: IOU and centroid distance
  • Initial implementation of topdown peak finder
  • Add tests for nn.paf_grouping.
  • Hotfix for TrainingJob/Model imports mess
  • Refactor InferenceModel to be simpler layer on top of a tf.keras.Model. - Can still be created from a TrainingJob using a factory class method. - Handles multi-inputs/outputs more generically - Less tied to TrainingJob/backbone construction, instead computes most properties implicitly. - Added a new utility method to replace .predict() without changing the input tensor to allow for arbitrary input sizes. This is handled implicitly now through the model's call() method.
  • Add peak-finding test for all zeros.
  • Revert local peak finding algorithm - New version didn't have a good way to deal with adjacent local peaks - Added iteration based method to deal with per-channel max filtering. This avoids moving a lot of memory on the GPU as with the old "channel packing" method, at the cost of more constraints on autograph since the channel dimension must be statically inferable. - Refactor ConfmapPeakFinder to batch above autographed functions calls.
  • Refactor which calls get autographed in centroid predictor - Now batching occurs above autographed functions to minimize retracing - Exposed more peak finding parameters at base class level
  • Check for training_job.json within model folder when deserializing
  • Typo in utils.VideoLoader.n_chunks property
  • Add integration test for nn.peak_finding.
  • Add tests for nn.peak_finding.
  • Bug fix for getting random prediction frames.
  • Command line utility for merging tracks.
  • Add test for velocity suggestions.
  • Add suggestioned frames based on high pt velocity.
  • Don't break when there are no tracks.
  • Add support for header series as numpy array.
  • Add hog feature suggestion, better bag of features
  • Disable contextual menu when clicking node.
  • Connect app state to instance overlay.
  • Add menu options for new displacement graph.
  • Fix input shape specification when building new backbones
  • Flow shift tracker refactoring - More faithful to original algorithm (no propagating shifted instances) - Matching options: Greedy or Hungarian - Generic instance similarity function - Use best shifted instance in the window by track ID - Filter shifted instances by optical flow scores and ignore instances with few points - Added tracking score field to PredictedInstances (good signal for proofreading) - Added image scaling as preprocessing for optical flow calculation
  • Add keras_applications-based network backbones. - Fix DenseNet implementation and add variants as well as generalized - Add MobileNetV1 and MobileNetV2 - Add NASNet Mobile, Large and generalized
  • PAF grouper refactoring
  • Add property to Skeleton to return edges as pairs of node indices
  • Add method for calc'ing single node displacement.
  • Fix merge conflicts
  • Divisibility check in resizing and extended batched call
  • Intermediate changes in inference modules overhaul - Peak finding redone - Centroid-based region proposals - Video loading via tf.data.Dataset - Initial tests stub via tf.test.TestCase unittests
  • Use bag of words for brisk, parallel per video.
  • Write tracks as tuples to save space.
  • Bug fix, connect state vars to color manager.
  • Sort groups by numeric rather than alpha order.
  • Moved suggestion pipeline into object.
  • Add test for brisk and pipeline.
  • Add default value for brisk thresh.
  • Add test to ensure hdf5 video import works.
  • Add test, remove debug print.
  • Refactor feature-based frame suggestions.
  • Suggestions grouped by video, interleave sortable.
  • Add group interleave sort option for suggestions.
  • Apply node/edge color to skeleton tables.
  • Initialize dicts in init so not shared.
  • Refactor gui coloring code.
  • conda install Pillow, block installing pyqt
  • Rename active learning gui test file.
  • Rename gui/active.py to gui/inference.py
  • Add support for instance placement methods.
  • Bug fixes for new suggestion format.
  • Remove code that was commented out.
  • Rename "active learning" to "training".
  • Re-enable prediction on suggested/random frames.
  • Mode drop-down menus in learning gui dialog.
  • Add black and pre-commit to dev reqs.
  • Store cluster/group id for suggestions.
  • Complete rewrite of core inference modules. - nn.peak_finding: - Global peak finding - Local peak finding via max filtering with threshold - Gaussian smoothing for suppressing spurious local peaks - Centered cropping - Local direction offset-based subpixel peak refinement - Autographed functions for fast graph mode execution of core ops - Generalized high level wrappers with lightweight eager mode control flow (e.g., different flags, defaults, variable outputs, etc.)
  • Enable Tensorboard through CLI and change default profile save path - Training job JSON now defaults to "{run_path}/training_job.json"
  • Use force-directed graph for node placement.
  • Don't die if ~/.sleap/ already exists.
  • Use config files stored in ~/.sleap/.

  • Active inference changes - Removed disk serialization of predictions (it was being overwritten after each video anyway) - Progress dialog works - Canceling works gracefully - Broken: asynchronous call so that the GUI remains responsive
  • Major refactoring of inference module - Created a InferenceModel class that holds all of the metadata required to run models from a TrainingJob. This includes input/output scale, skeletons, Keras model loading and caching, variable input shape, normalization and other convenience properties. - Added some generic attributes to the sleap.nn.Model class to allow for storing or inferring output scaling (depends on architecture details, so it can be overloaded in backbones). - sleap.nn.loadmodel module removed. - Predictor refactored to take advantage of InferenceModel caching and reuse multiprocessing pool (fixes: #155). - Previously, models were being reloaded from disk and copies made every time a new crop size was encountered. - Previously, a new pool was being opened on every predict() call. - Refactored DataTransform usage to account for discrepancies between input scale vs output scale vs relative output scale vs scale across models. - Fix: DataTransform-based scaling would always convert images to float64 dtype. - Refactored TensorFlow-based peak finding for GPU execution. - Account for unspecified confmaps tensor at graph creation time. - Fix subpixel refinement algorithm to enable greater resolution than original data scale. - General rework of offset adjustment. - Exposed supersampling parameters (window size, window batching) - Fix tensors being overwritten or dropping out of context - Fixes: #149 - Exposed peak finding options and other inference functionality as Predictor attributes. - Renamed: Instance.visible_points_array -> Instance.points_array - Sensible cropping defaults in profiles and function kwargs. - Indented default profiles for readability. - General unfucking of syntax errors, anti-patterns, typos, etc.

docs.title: Our Pledge
docs.text: In the interest of fostering an open and welcoming environment, we as contributors and maintainers pledge to make participation in our project and our community a harassment-free experience for everyone, regardless of age, body size, disability, ethnicity, gender identity and expression, level of experience, nationality, personal appearance, race, religion, or sexual identity and orientation.

docs.title: Our Standards
docs.text:
Examples of behavior that contributes to creating a positive environment include:

  • Using welcoming and inclusive language
  • Being respectful of differing viewpoints and experiences
  • Gracefully accepting constructive criticism
  • Focusing on what is best for the community
  • Showing empathy towards other community members

Examples of unacceptable behavior by participants include:

  • The use of sexualized language or imagery and unwelcome sexual attention or advances
  • Trolling, insulting/derogatory comments, and personal or political attacks
  • Public or private harassment
  • Publishing others' private information, such as a physical or electronic address, without explicit permission
  • Other conduct which could reasonably be considered inappropriate in a professional setting

docs.title: Our Responsibilities
docs.text:
Project maintainers are responsible for clarifying the standards of acceptable behavior and are expected to take appropriate and fair corrective action in response to any instances of unacceptable behavior.

Project maintainers have the right and responsibility to remove, edit, or reject comments, commits, code, wiki edits, issues, and other contributions that are not aligned to this Code of Conduct, or to ban temporarily or permanently any contributor for other behaviors that they deem inappropriate, threatening, offensive, or harmful.

docs.title: Scope
docs.text: This Code of Conduct applies both within project spaces and in public spaces when an individual is representing the project or its community. Examples of representing a project or community include using an official project e-mail address, posting via an official social media account, or acting as an appointed representative at an online or offline event. Representation of a project may be further defined and clarified by project maintainers.

docs.title: Enforcement
docs.text:
Instances of abusive, harassing, or otherwise unacceptable behavior may be reported by contacting the project team at talmo@salk.edu. All complaints will be reviewed and investigated and will result in a response that is deemed necessary and appropriate to the circumstances. The project team is obligated to maintain confidentiality with regard to the reporter of an incident. Further details of specific enforcement policies may be posted separately.

Project maintainers who do not follow or enforce the Code of Conduct in good faith may face temporary or permanent repercussions as determined by other members of the project's leadership.

docs.title: Attribution
docs.text: This Code of Conduct is adapted from the Contributor Covenant, version 1.4, available at https://contributor-covenant.org/version/1/4

docs.title: Contributing to SLEAP
docs.text: As our community grows it is important to adhere to a set of contribution guidelines. These guidelines may change as needed. Please feel free to propose changes to source code in a pull request!

docs.title: Code of Conduct
docs.text: Everyone contributing to SLEAP is governed by the SLEAP Code of Conduct. As such, all are expected to abide by this code. Please report any unacceptable behavior to talmo@salk.edu.

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text: Github has made it easy to separate issues from discussions. Generally speaking, if something warrants a pull request (e.g. bug fixes, TODOs) it should be submitted as an issue. Conversely, general questions about usage and improvement ideas should go into discussions. These both act as staging areas before adding the items to the SLEAP Roadmap. Once added to the Roadmap, items can be given priority, status, assignee(s), etc.

docs.title: Issues
docs.text:
• Check open/closed issues, ideas, and the help page to make sure issue doesn't already exist / has been solved.
  • Create new issue using the issue template.

docs.title: Discussions
docs.text:
• This is a place to go to ask questions and propose new ideas.
  • 3 categories: Help, Ideas, General
  • Help - Having trouble with software, user experience issue, etc.
  • Ideas - Enhancements, things that would make the user experience nicer but not necessarily a problem with the code.
  • General - If it doesn't fall into help/ideas it goes here as long as it isn't bug fix (issue).

docs.title: Pull Requests
docs.text:
1. Install source code develop branch and follow instructions to create conda env, etc.
  2. Create a fork from the develop branch.
  3. Either work on the develop branch or create a new branch (recommended if tackling multiple issues at a time).
  4. If creating a branch, use your name followed by a relevant keyword for your changes, eg: git checkout -b john/some_issue
  5. Make some changes/additions to the source code that tackle the issue(s).
  6. Write tests.
  7. Can either write tests before creating a draft PR, or submit draft PR (to get code coverage statistics via codecov) and then write tests to narrow down error prone lines.
  8. The test(s) should go into relevant subtest folders to the proposed change(s).
  9. Test(s) should aim to hit every point in the proposed change(s) - cover edge cases to best of your ability.
  10. Try to hit code coverage points.
  11. Add files, commit, and push to origin.
  12. Create a draft PR on Github (follow instructions in template).
  13. Make sure the tests pass and code coverage is good.
  14. If either the tests or code coverage fail, repeat steps 3-5.
  15. Once the draft PR looks good, convert to a finalized PR (hit the ready for review button).
  16. IMPORTANT: Only convert to a finalized PR when you believe your changes are ready to be merged.
  17. Optionally assign a reviewer on the right of the screen - otherwise a member of the SLEAP developer team will self-assign themselves.
  18. If the reviewer requests changes, repeat steps 3-5 and Re-request review.
  19. Once the reviewer signs off they will squash + merge the PR into the develop branch.
  20. New feautures will be available on the main branch when a new release of SLEAP is released.

docs.title: Style Guides
docs.text:
• Lint - Black version 21.6b0 (see dev_requirements for any changes).
  • Code - Generally follow PEP8. Type hinting is encouraged.
  • Documentation - Use Google-style comments and docstrings to document code.

docs.title: Thank you for contributing to SLEAP!
Help
docs.text: Stuck? Can't get SLEAP to run? Crashing? Try the recommended tips below.

docs.title: I can't get SLEAP to install!
docs.text:
Have you tried all of the steps in the installation instructions?

If so, please feel free to start a discussion or open an issue and tell us how you're trying to install it, what error messages you're getting and which operating system you're on.

docs.title: Can I install it on a computer without a GPU?
docs.text: Yes! You can install SLEAP as you normally would using the conda or pip-based methods and the GPU support will be ignored.

docs.title: What if I already have CUDA set up on my system?
docs.text:
You can use the system CUDA installation by simply using the pip package installation method.

Note that you will need to use a version compatible with TensorFlow 2.6+ (CUDA Toolkit v11.3 and cuDNN v8.2).

docs.title: How do I use SLEAP?
docs.text:
If you're new to pose tracking in general, check out this talk or our review in Nature Neuroscience.

If you're just new to SLEAP, we suggest starting with the high-level overview and then following the tutorial.

Once you get the hang of it, check out the guides for more detailed info.

docs.title: Does my data need to be in a particular format?
docs.text:
SLEAP supports a large number of formats, including all video formats and imported data from DeepLabCut and others.

Many types of video acquisition software, however, do not save videos in a format suitable for computer vision-based processing. A very common issue is that videos are not reliably seekable, meaning that you may not get the same data when trying to read a particular frame index. This is because many video formats are optimized for realtime and sequential playback and save space by reconstructing the image using data in adjacent frames. The consequence is that you may not get the exact same image depending on what the last frame you read was. Check out this blog post for more details.

If you think you may be affected by this issue, or just want to be safe, re-encode your videos using the following command:

ffmpeg -y -i "input.mp4" -c:v libx264 -pix_fmt yuv420p -preset superfast -crf 23 "output.mp4"

Breaking down what this does:

  • -i "input.mp4": Specifies the path to the input file. Replace this with your video. Can be .avi or any other video format.
  • -c:v libx264: Sets the video compression to use H264.
  • -pix_fmt yuv420p: Necessary for playback on some systems.
  • -preset superfast: Sets a number of parameters that enable reliable seeking.
  • -crf 23: Sets the quality of the output video. Lower numbers are less lossy, but result in larger files. A CRF of 15 is nearly lossless, while 30 will be highly compressed.
  • "output.mp4": The name of the output video file (must end in .mp4).

If you don't have ffmpeg on your system, you can install it using conda install ffmpeg or by downloading it from the official website.

docs.title: I get strange results where the poses appear to be correct but shifted relative to the image.
docs.text: This is most likely an issue with the video compression format. See above.

docs.title: How do I get predictions out?
docs.text: See export-analysis and sleap-convert.

docs.title: What do I do with the output of SLEAP?
docs.text: Check out the Analysis examples notebooks.

docs.title: I've found a bug or have another problem!
docs.text: Feel free to start a discussion to get help from the developers and community. Or open an issue and we'll get back to you as soon as we can!

docs.title: Can I just talk to someone?
docs.text:
SLEAP is a complex machine learning system intended for general use, so it's possible that we failed to consider the specifics of the situation in which you may be interested in using it with.

Feel free to reach out to us at talmo@salk.edu if you have a question that isn't covered here.

docs.title: How can I help improve SLEAP?
docs.text:
• Tell your friends about SLEAP! We also love to hear stories about what worked or didn't work, or your experience if you came from other software tools (talmo@salk.edu).

  • Cite our paper! Here's a BibTeX citation for your reference manager:

docs.text:
